title: Bauxite Trade
summary:
Main
By Yiannis Parganas
Recent developments in the Guinea bauxite trade are introducing a more complex outlook for the Capesize segment, as the interaction between freight costs, commodity pricing and government policy begins to influence trade flows on one of the most important long-haul bulk routes. Guinea has become the dominant supplier in the global bauxite market, with exports reaching about 183 million tonnes in 2025, a yearly increase of roughly 25%. The majority of these volumes moved to China, which continues to rely heavily on imported feedstock to support aluminium production close to its effective capacity ceiling of around 45-46 million tonnes per year.
